subject: English
title: The Last Leaf – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The story explores the connection between mental strength and physical recovery
Johnsy’s illness is worsened by her loss of hope
Sue’s emotional support plays an important role in her recovery
Behrman’s final act transforms him from a failed artist into a true hero
The painted leaf becomes the turning point in the story
The ending reveals that Behrman’s masterpiece was created through sacrifice
characters: Johnsy – Represents the human struggle between despair and hope
Sue – Represents compassion, loyalty, and encouragement
Behrman – Represents artistic dedication and ultimate sacrifice
themes: The power of hope and positive thinking
Selfless sacrifice for the welfare of others
The influence of art on human emotions
The relationship between mind and physical health
literary_devices: Symbolism – The leaf symbolizes life and resilience
Irony – Behrman’s masterpiece is not recognized during his life
Foreshadowing – His dream of painting a masterpiece predicts the ending
Contrast – Johnsy’s despair contrasts with Sue’s optimism
exam_tips: Analyse the symbolism of the ivy leaf
Explain Behrman’s role as a tragic hero
Discuss the psychological aspect of Johnsy’s illness
Link the story’s events with the themes of hope and sacrifice
